findings.description: 12 phantom sections with empty titles exist in the knowledge file but have no corresponding source sections. The source has 15 meaningful sections (intro + 14 subsections under アルファベット順/五十音順), yielding an expected section count of ~15. The knowledge file has 27 sections instead, with these 12 filler sections containing only '（対象用語なし）' and hints like '対象用語なし'. They inflate the index without adding any content. Note: s21 (ま行), s23 (や行), s27 (わ行) are correctly short because the source explicitly states '対象用語なし' for those rows, so only the 12 titleless phantom sections are at issue.
findings.source_evidence: Source RST defines exactly 14 subsection headings (A～G, H～N, O～U, V～Z, あ行, か行, さ行, た行, な行, は行, ま行, や行, ら行, わ行) plus the document intro. No source sections exist between A～G and H～N, between H～N and O～U, etc. that would justify s3, s5, s7, s9, s11, s13, s15, s18, s20, s22, s24, s26.
